summary.intent: Reduce the overwhelming number of models shown in the LLM provider settings and onboarding UI, and improve the visibility of probe errors when a model fails validation.
summary.outcome: Implemented collapsible model lists (top 3 visible by default with expand toggle), added recommended/preferred model sorting, added a `recommended` field to `ModelInfo` with smart flagging, hid legacy models (>1 year old) from chat selector, fixed probe error messages showing generic text instead of actual server errors, and added version-number-based sorting for models without dates.

repo: Two separate biome versions are in use: `npx biome` (0.3.3) vs system `biome` via mise (2.4.6). The validation script uses the system version which has stricter ternary formatting rules — always use system biome for pre-commit checks.

The WS connect handler in ws-connect.js line 96 calls `localizeRpcError` on ALL error responses, replacing server error messages with locale strings keyed by error code. This meant 'You do not have permission to access X' was replaced by 'Service temporarily unavailable'.
All non-Forbidden `ServiceError`s get serialized with code `UNAVAILABLE` in service-traits/src/lib.rs, which triggers the generic locale message replacement.
There are three separate model card rendering code paths: page-providers.js (settings), onboarding-view.js (onboarding), and providers.js (Preferred Models modal — imperative DOM, not Preact).
The `models.list_all` RPC (used by settings) previously did not include the `preferred` field, only `models.list` did.
Static model catalogs use a tuple format `(model_id, display_name, created_at)` — the `catalog_to_discovered` helper marks the first N entries as recommended.
OpenAI API-discovered models go through `parse_model_entry` in openai.rs where flagship patterns can be matched for the `recommended` flag.
The local-validate.sh script checks i18n parity across 3 locales and 18 namespaces automatically.

code.finding: All ServiceError::Message variants serialize to error code UNAVAILABLE, causing frontend locale to overwrite the actual error message
frame.error = localizeRpcError(frame.error) runs on every WS response, replacing server messages with locale strings before resolving promises
localizedRpcErrorMessage returns locale string by code, ignoring server-provided message — fixed to preserve serverMessage on error object
parse_model_entry builds DiscoveredModel from OpenAI API response — good place to pattern-match flagship model IDs for recommended flag
Legacy model hiding (>1 year) implemented in list() but not list_all() — preferred models and models without timestamps are always exempt

workflow: Run `biome` (system, from mise) not `npx biome` for formatting — the versions have incompatible ternary formatting rules.
After adding a new field to a Rust struct, a bulk sed approach to add default values to test blocks is faster than fixing one by one, but requires care to avoid modifying non-target structs.
The local-validate.sh script is the authoritative check before merging — catches biome version mismatches that local `cargo fmt` and `npx biome` won't.

friction: Three separate model card rendering code paths (settings, onboarding, preferred models modal) meant the same fix had to be applied three times — the third was missed initially.
Biome version mismatch (npx 0.3.3 vs system 2.4.6) caused formatting failures in CI that passed local lint checks, requiring a separate fix commit.

Adding `recommended: bool` to ModelInfo required touching ~15 construction sites across multiple files plus test blocks — bulk sed was used but incorrectly modified a non-ModelInfo struct (PendingInvoke) in server.rs.
The catalog_to_discovered helper existed but was private — had to be made pub to use from provider submodules.

The probe error message being overwritten by locale strings was a three-layer problem: ServiceError code → localizeRpcError → locale lookup, requiring changes in both Rust (to preserve detail) and JS (to expose serverMessage).
open_items: Popularity-based model sorting before first use was deferred — suggested approach is adding a `recommended` tier/field to ModelInfo in the Rust provider code (done), but crowdsourced or cross-instance popularity data is not implemented.
The `humanizeProbeError` function in provider-validation.js still has some generic patterns that may be too broad — the 'permission' pattern was partially fixed but full audit was not done.
The `providers.show_legacy_models` config option was added but there is no UI toggle — users must edit moltis.toml directly.
GitHub Copilot and OpenAI-compatible providers have top-N recommended flagging but no pattern-based flagship detection like OpenAI's `parse_model_entry` approach.
The collapse/expand state in the settings page resets on page navigation — no persistence of expanded state was implemented.
